primary_concern: Sepsis with multi-organ dysfunction and acute kidney injury (AKI) progression
clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) combined with leukocytosis (14.2 K/uL), thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL), and hemoglobin of 9.8 g/dL meet Sepsis-3 criteria for sepsis risk. Bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) suggests hepatic involvement. AKI progressed from Stage 1 (2.1 mg/dL) to Stage 3 (8.9 mg/dL) creatinine within 48 hours, though the Stage 3 value was excluded as an outlier. Platelets remain persistently below 100 K/uL, indicating SOFA coagulation score ≥1.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and AKI meet Sepsis-3 criteria for sepsis
prioritized_risks.threshold: Suspected infection plus acute SOFA increase ≥2

prioritized_risks.explanation: Creatinine rose from baseline to 2.1 mg/dL (Stage 1), with excluded outlier at 8.9 mg/dL
prioritized_risks.threshold: Creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h or ≥1.5x baseline in 7 days

prioritized_risks.explanation: Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coagulation score threshold

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per sepsis protocol
Order blood cultures and infection-focused imaging
Monitor fluid resuscitation response and consider vasopressors if hypotension develops
Evaluate for AKI reversal strategies (e.g., nephrotoxin review, diuretic optimization)
Consider platelet transfusion if <50 K/uL or active bleeding present
Repeat lactate and CBC in 4-6 hours to assess trend
doctor_handoff: Review for source control of infection, confirm AKI etiology (contrast, sepsis, ATN), and assess need for hemodialysis given Stage 3 AKI. Monitor for septic shock progression with MAP and vasopressor requirements.

disease_progression.observation: Initial sepsis markers: WBC 14.2 K/uL, Hgb 9.8 g/dL, Bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL
AKI Stage 3 (Cr 8.9 mg/dL excluded as outlier), Platelets 88 K/uL

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
